batch

Class BatchConfiguration

  • java.lang.Object
    • batch.BatchConfiguration

  • public final class BatchConfiguration
extends java.lang.Object
    An immutable configuration object representing the validated parameters for a batch processing operation.

    This class provides a snapshot of settings produced by BatchBuilder after validation has been completed. Once created, the configuration cannot be modified.

    Since:
    2 June 2026
    Version:
    1.0
    Author:
    Trevor Maggs

      private java.nio.file.Path target

      Creates an immutable configuration from validated builder state.

      All Methods Instance Methods Concrete Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method Description
      java.util.Set<java.lang.String> getFileSet()
      Returns the set of filenames explicitly selected for processing.

      Returns the filename prefix to prepend to generated filenames.

      Returns the source directory containing the media files to be processed.
      java.nio.file.Path getTarget()
      Returns the destination directory for processed files.
      java.time.ZonedDateTime getUserDate()
      Returns the user-defined timestamp used for metadata updates.
      boolean isDebug()
      Indicates whether diagnostic output is enabled.
      boolean isDescending()
      Indicates whether media files should be processed in descending chronological order.
      boolean isEmbedDateTime()
      Indicates whether timestamps should be embedded in generated filenames.
      boolean isForceDateChange()
      Indicates whether existing metadata date values should be overwritten.
      boolean isShowMetadata()
      Indicates whether detailed metadata information should be displayed.
      boolean isSkipVideo()
      Indicates whether video files should be excluded from processing.

    • Constructor Detail

      • BatchConfiguration

        BatchConfiguration(java.nio.file.Path source,
                   java.nio.file.Path target,
                   java.lang.String prefix,
                   java.time.ZonedDateTime userDate,
                   java.lang.String[] fileSet,
                   boolean forceDateChange,
                   boolean embedDateTime,
                   boolean skipVideo,
                   boolean showMetadata,
                   boolean descending,
                   boolean debug)
        Creates an immutable configuration from validated builder state.

        This constructor is intended for use by BatchBuilder after all configuration values have been validated.

        Parameters:
        source - the directory containing the original media files
        target - the destination directory for processed copies
        prefix - a user-defined string prepended to new filenames
        userDate - a user-defined timestamp for metadata updates, or null
        fileSet - an optional array containing the filenames to process
        forceDateChange - flag to overwrite existing metadata tags
        embedDateTime - flag to include timestamps in the generated filename
        skipVideo - flag to ignore video formats during processing
        showMetadata - flag to display detailed metadata information
        descending - flag to sort media in reverse chronological order
        debug - flag to enable verbose diagnostic output

    • Method Detail

      • getSource

        public java.nio.file.Path getSource()
        Returns the source directory containing the media files to be processed.
        Returns:
        the source directory

      • getTarget

        public java.nio.file.Path getTarget()
        Returns the destination directory for processed files.
        Returns:
        the target directory

      • getPrefix

        public java.lang.String getPrefix()
        Returns the filename prefix to prepend to generated filenames.
        Returns:
        the configured filename prefix, or an empty string if no prefix was specified

      • getUserDate

        public java.time.ZonedDateTime getUserDate()
        Returns the user-defined timestamp used for metadata updates.
        Returns:
        the configured timestamp, or null if original file dates should be used

      • getFileSet

        public java.util.Set<java.lang.String> getFileSet()
        Returns the set of filenames explicitly selected for processing.
        Returns:
        an unmodifiable set of filenames, or an empty set if no file filter was specified

      • isForceDateChange

        public boolean isForceDateChange()
        Indicates whether existing metadata date values should be overwritten.
        Returns:
        true if existing metadata dates should be replaced, false otherwise

      • isEmbedDateTime

        public boolean isEmbedDateTime()
        Indicates whether timestamps should be embedded in generated filenames.
        Returns:
        true if timestamps should be included in filenames, false otherwise

      • isSkipVideo

        public boolean isSkipVideo()
        Indicates whether video files should be excluded from processing.
        Returns:
        true if video files should be skipped, false otherwise

      • isShowMetadata

        public boolean isShowMetadata()
        Indicates whether detailed metadata information should be displayed.
        Returns:
        true if detailed metadata information should be displayed, false otherwise

      • isDescending

        public boolean isDescending()
        Indicates whether media files should be processed in descending chronological order.
        Returns:
        true if descending ordering is enabled, false otherwise

      • isDebug

        public boolean isDebug()
        Indicates whether diagnostic output is enabled.
        Returns:
        true if debug mode is enabled, false otherwise
